mnemonics: A GROUP Has Identity, Inverses, Closure, and is Associative (GHIICA)
keyPoints: A group is a set equipped with a binary operation that is associative, has an identity element, and every element has an inverse.
The order of a group is the number of elements in its set.
Groups are the fundamental algebraic structures used to study symmetry.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to check for closure under the binary operation.
Assuming commutativity (that the operation is abelian) when it is not a required group property.
Confusing the identity element (e.g., using 1 for an additive group or 0 for a multiplicative group).
